data.title: ClearBridge Large Cap Growth Strategy Q4: Adds Arista Networks, exits Starbucks, shifts restaurant exposure to Chipotle
data.description:
• The ClearBridge Large Cap Growth Strategy lagged the Russell 1000 Growth Index during a major market rally, mainly because it held fewer of the biggest AI stocks and avoided lower-quality AI-related companies.
  • It opened a position in Arista Networks (ANET), citing the company’s relatively small share in the enterprise segment and its strong potential to gain ground as organizations modernize their campus networking infrastructure.
  • It added that it will continue to be active in adding to positions or initiating new ones during pullbacks, such as it did with Palo Alto Networks (PANW) following its announcement of the CyberArk acquisition and Netflix (NFLX) when the stock sold off following disclosure of its intent to purchase Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD).
  • The strategy exited Accenture (ACN) in Q3 and sold Starbucks (SBUX) to focus its restaurant exposure on Chipotle (CMG).
     
  • Source: Press Relase

data.title: Solid Bio rises as Duchenne study gets fully enrolled
data.description:
Solid Biosciences (SLDB) traded higher on Wednesday after the gene therapy developer announced full enrollment in a Phase 1/2 study for its lead candidate SGT-003 in children with a muscle-wasting disorder called Duchenne muscular dystrophy.

The trial named INSPIRE DUCHENNE was previously expected to enroll 50–60 patients across clinical trial sites in the U.S., Canada, Italy, and the U.K.

“We're going to be notifying the sites, hopefully, in the next month or 2 that we're basically not going to take any more recruitment because we filled every possible slot that's possible,” said CEO Alexander Cumbo during the company’s presentation at the JPMorgan Healthcare Conference on Tuesday.

Additionally, Solid Bio (SLDB) provided an interim update from the study noting that as of Jan. 9, SGT-003 has been generally well tolerated among 33 patients who received dosing, adding to a previous safety update that represented 23 patients as of Oct. 31, 2025.

Additional data from the study, as well as an update on the company’s interactions with the FDA regarding a Phase 3 confirmatory trial design for SGT-003, are expected in mid-2026.

data.title: Gevo awarded patent for ethanol-to-olefins process to produce renewable jet fuel
data.description:
Gevo (GEVO) +2.2% in Wednesday's trading after saying it was awarded a patent for its ethanol-to-olefins technology to produce renewable jet fuel and chemicals, potentially lowering operating costs by as much as 35% compared to current technologies.

Gevo (GEVO) said the patented process produces light olefins from ethanol and can convert those olefins into transportation fuels using commercially proven alcohol-to-jet technologies; olefins can be used to make cost-effective renewable chemical building blocks such as bio-propylene.

Gevo (GEVO) is developing ethanol-to-olefins technology with partners LG Chem to deploy a next-generation ETO process for renewable chemical applications and with Axens for fuel applications.

"We continue to build an intellectual property portfolio that we believe will put Gevo in a cost-leadership position on [alcohol-to-jet technologies] for years to come," Gevo (GEVO) President Paul Bloom said.

data.title: Virtu, Optiver partner to challenge Citadel Securities in US options flow - report
data.description:
Virtu Financial (VIRT), Akuna Capital, Belvedere Trading, and Optiver are investing in a firm that will buy U.S. options order flow from retail brokers, a market dominated by Ken Griffin's Citadel Securities, according to a media report on Wednesday.

The four companies have invested in a new options intermediary, Optimal Market Technologies, Bloomberg News reported, citing people familiar with the matter. The four market makers will lead a larger group of liquidity providers, who will compete for flow routed from trading apps used by small-scale traders to Optimal, the article said.

Optimal has received approval from the Financial Industry Regulatory Authority for this system. But it hasn't yet received final approval from the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission to create an alternative trading system, an electronic platform that matches buyers with sellers.

U.S. retail brokers, such as Robinhood Markets (HOOD) and Morgan Stanley's (MS) E*Trade — which cut their trading commissions to as low as zero — make most of their money by selling their customers orders to wholesalers. Order flow payments across equities and options climbed 51% to $1.24B in Q3 2025 from a year earlier, according to analysis from Bloomberg Intelligence.

data.title: Impinj sells off even after strong guidance update
data.description:
• Impinj (PI) shares fell more than 6% in late morning trading on Wednesday, even as the company provided a guidance update for its fourth-quarter that was stronger-than-expected. 
  • The Seattle-based company now expects fourth-quarter revenue to come in near the high end of its prior $90Mto $93M guidance range, compared to the analyst estimate of $91.9M. Impinj also said it expects adjusted EBITDA to exceed the midpoint of its previously issued $15.4M–16.9M guidance.
  • Chris Diorio, Impinj co-founder and CEO, and Cary Baker, Impinj CFO, are appearing at a Needham investment conference on Wednesday.
  • The company is set to report its full-year results for the fourth quarter and full year ended December 31, 2025, on February 5.

data.title: Winners and losers from the November Retail Sales Report
data.description:
Retail sales were up 0.6% month-over-month in November and were 3.3% higher than a year ago, according to the U.S. Census Bureau. The retail sales growth was higher than the 0.4% increase economists projected. Core retail sales, which feed into the calculation for consumer spending for GDP for the quarter, rose 0.4% after improving by a downwardly revised 0.6% in October.

Sales were up across most categories for the beginning of the holiday season. Clothing stores stood out with a 7.5% year-over-year jump in a positive sign for mall retailers such as Gap (GAP), Urban Outfitters (URBN), American Eagle Outfitters (AEO), and Abercrombie & Fitch (ANF). Once again, the health & personal care stores category was strong with 6.7% growth, which may bode well for chains such as Walgreens, Rite Aid, Ulta Beauty (ULTA), Sally Beauty (SBH), and National Vision Holdings (EYE). The nonstore retailers category outperformed with a 7.2% increase in November from a year ago. The holiday month is important for e-commerce players included in the category, such as Amazon (AMZN), Wayfair (W), and Etsy (ETSY).

The auto dealer group was an underperformer in November, with a 1.1% decline from a year ago. Some names included in that tally are Carvana (CVNA), CarMax (KMX), Lithia Motors (LAD), AutoNation (AN), Asbury Automotive Group (ABG), CarGurus (CARG), Group 1 Automotive (GPI), Sonic Automotive (SAH), and Penske Automotive Group (PAG)

"Altogether, it's a fairly solid report pointing to a resilient consumer despite signs of eroding consumer confidence, which has dropped to recession levels, worries about inflation, and a slowing labor market," summarized KPMG Senior Economist Ken Kim on the report.

data.title: U.S. stocks see continued declines as traders digest PPI, retail sales
data.description:
Wall Street's major averages saw another day of declines on Wednesday after the wholesale inflation data came in line with consensus, while retail sales were hotter than expected.

The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) was last -0.8% in late morning trade, while the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was -1.3%, and the Dow (DJI) was -0.3%.

The U.S. Producer Price Index rose in line with consensus in November, while retail sales jumped past consensus for that month.

Moreover, both business inventories and retail inventories in October rose higher than expected, according to the U.S. Census Bureau.

“Wednesday's PPI, while delayed, helps to confirm that the inflation data remained stable even during the data drought of the government shutdown and helps to justify the Federal Reserve's rate cut in December, which was initiated largely because of softening jobs data rather than worries about inflation,” said Clark Bellin, president and CIO at Bellwether Wealth.

“Stocks are off to an encouraging start so far in 2026, even with the barrage of political and geopolitical headlines out of Washington. Volatility remains subdued even with a market that is facing seemingly endless headlines, which shows that investors have largely become desensitized to the day-to-day movements out of Washington,” Bellin added.

In addition, the current account deficit narrowed more than expected in Q3 2025. 

Lastly, U.S. existing home sales rose 5.1% MoM to a seasonally adjusted annual rate of 4.35M in December vs. 4.230M consensus and 4.14M prior, the National Association of Realtors said.

Over in the bond market, Treasury yields moved lower across the curve, with the benchmark 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) 4 basis points lower to 4.14%, while the 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) 4 basis points lower to 3.51%.

The U.S. Supreme Court was once again expected to release a ruling on the legality of President Donald Trump’s tariffs, but there was no ruling issued.

On earnings, Wells Fargo (WFC) -4.6% reported weaker than expected revenue for the fourth quarter, while Bank of America (BAC) topped Wall Street expectations, still -4.4%.

“We believe the upcoming tech earnings reports at the end of January and early February will be the most important in recent memory. Tech was the best performing sector of 2025 and the guidance that tech companies release in the coming weeks will be important to determine whether or not tech can have another banner year in 2026,” Bellin said.

More on markets

data.title: American Honda expects to sell 4% more vehicles in 2026
data.description:
As the auto industry scales down production of purely electric vehicles, Honda (HMC) said it is committed to offering the “right mix of gas-powered and hybrid vehicles to meet customer demand for affordable, high-value products.”  

And with nearly all of its Honda and Acura models redesigned, American Honda (HMC) expects to sell 1.5M vehicles in 2026, an increase of 4% from last year. In 2025, the company sold 1.43M, up 0.5% from 2024, marking the company’s best year since 2021.

For 2026, the company is maintaining a flexible and balanced strategy to remain competitive and “adapt to evolving market conditions” by increasing production of core Honda models like the CR-V, Civic, and Accord, along with the Acura ADX and Integra.

Among the improvements to its lineup, a refreshed 2026 Honda Pilot will begin arriving at dealerships in mid-December, equipped with a larger touchscreen and other upgrades and safety features. The S+ Shift feature, introduced on the Prelude, will be added to the Civic later this year.

For its luxury Acura brand, the RSX electrified SUV will arrive in H2 2026, while the Type S and MDX will see product enhancements later this year. Additionally, the next-generation RDX—currently under development—will feature a two-motor hybrid system.

data.title: Rocky Mountain Chocolate Factory outlines $500,000–$1M cost savings potential as margin-first transformation accelerates
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Rocky Mountain Chocolate Factory (RMCF) Q3 2026

Management View

  • Interim CEO Jeffrey Geygan described the quarter as an “inflection point” in the company’s margin-first transformation strategy, emphasizing deliberate decisions to prioritize profitability and long-term value over lower quality revenue. Geygan stated, “While these actions resulted in a near-term revenue pressure and a modest net loss for the quarter, they're foundational to restoring long-term sustainable growth and shareholder value creation.”
  • Geygan reported operational progress, including a gross manufacturing margin of 21.4% for the quarter ended November 30, 2025, compared to 10% for the same quarter in the prior year and a negative 0.6% for the previous quarter. He outlined targeted price adjustments, SKU rationalization, and labor efficiencies, noting, “We believe there's an additional $500,000 to $1 million of savings that can be realized in our current cost structure.”
  • The franchise development pipeline is gaining momentum, with 2 new stores under construction and 34 stores under area development agreements. Geygan said, “Our franchise development team is working on building additional backlog of new franchise opportunities supported by our clear messaging with a refreshed brand direction and targeted digital marketing efforts.”
  • The company’s digital strategy advanced with all stores now having unique websites and the rollout of DoorDash storefronts, along with further POS system adoption and ERP improvements.
  • CFO Carrie Cass stated, “For the fiscal third quarter of '26, total revenue was $7.5 million compared to $7.9 million in the prior year. Discipline reflects our intentional exit from low or negative margin revenue streams as part of our margin first strategy. Total product and retail gross profit increased to $1.4 million in the third quarter of fiscal '26 compared to $0.7 million in the same quarter last year, driven by pricing actions, improved product mix and labor efficiencies.”

Outlook

  • Management expects continued margin expansion, supported by lower input costs, including the recent elimination of a 10% tariff on cocoa and favorable purchasing strategies that locked in nearly 20% of expected annual consumption volume at recent lower prices. Geygan explained that these efforts are “expected to support margin expansion over time in a balanced way that enables strong economic results.”
  • The franchise growth pipeline is expected to accelerate as new agreements mature, with remodels for most stores scheduled to be completed by October 2026. Geygan reiterated, “We expect our disciplined approach to area development and franchisee recruitment will drive meaningful long-term results for our network performance.”

Financial Results

  • Gross manufacturing margin reached 21.4% for the quarter ended November 30, 2025, a notable increase from the previous year’s 10% and prior quarter’s negative 0.6%.
  • Total revenue for the quarter was $7.5 million, down from $7.9 million in the prior year, reflecting the exit from low-margin revenue streams.
  • Product and retail gross profit rose to $1.4 million from $0.7 million last year. Total costs and expenses improved to $7.5 million, down from $8.6 million.
  • Net loss narrowed to ($0.2 million) or (-$0.02) per share, compared to a net loss of ($0.8 million) or (-$0.11) per share last year. EBITDA was $0.4 million versus a negative $0.4 million a year ago.

Q&A

  • Doug Garber, Westport Alpha: Asked about the pace of deployment for 34 new stores, financing, cocoa price impact on margins, and the balance sheet recapitalization. Geygan responded that the area development agreements are “across 4 unique franchisees... rollout of these will be on a measured basis, but accelerating into the later years.” He also stated, “We were able to lock it in at closer to $5,000 for roughly 20% of our expected production this year,” and expects a margin tailwind from lower cocoa prices. On the balance sheet, Geygan said the focus is on “reducing debt, investing in the company, all of which we presume will be coming from free cash flow as opposed to additional equity issuance.”
  • Peter Sidoti, Sidoti & Company, Inc.: Asked when the accelerated franchise effort would impact the top line and about obstacles to growth. Geygan noted a lag due to ramp-up time for new stores and explained, “We would have very little interest in supporting the opening of a store that we don't think can generate at least $1 million in annual sales at retail over 3 years.” On obstacles, he cited execution, stating, “We just -- we need to do a better job at executing profitably...this isn't a cost savings story. This is a top line story.”

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts pressed for clarity on growth timing, margin tailwinds, and capital strategy, reflecting a neutral to slightly positive tone, with repeated attention to execution and profitability.
  • Management maintained a confident but measured tone, emphasizing “disciplined execution,” “intentional” strategy, and “progress,” while frequently citing ongoing work and room for improvement. Geygan’s language included, “We believe there is room for further improvement,” reflecting cautious optimism.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, both analysts and management maintained a similar focus on transformation and profitability, but the current call included more concrete progress and specific margin expansion figures.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The current quarter highlighted a recovery in gross margin (21.4% vs. negative 0.6% in Q2), narrowing net loss, and realization of cost savings, while the previous quarter focused on foundational changes and new leadership.
  • Franchise pipeline detail advanced from early-stage agreements in Q2 to now 34 area development agreements in Q3.
  • Management’s tone moved from emphasizing foundational work to discussing tangible results, while analysts shifted from queries about leadership background and capital needs to more forward-looking questions on execution and growth impact.
  • Strategic focus evolved from modernizing operations and brand repositioning to measured franchise expansion, margin expansion, and technology adoption.

Risks and Concerns

  • Persistently higher input costs and near-term production inefficiencies remain challenges, though management expects margin support from lower cocoa prices and operational efficiencies.
  • Execution risk is top of mind, as cited by Geygan: “We just -- we need to do a better job at executing profitably.”
  • Analysts also raised concerns about the lag between new store openings and top-line impact, and the balance between cost controls and revenue growth.

Final Takeaway

Rocky Mountain Chocolate Factory management emphasized that the company’s margin-first transformation is delivering tangible improvements in profitability and operational efficiency, with clear momentum in franchise development and digital initiatives. While headwinds remain, management signaled confidence in further margin expansion, cost savings, and a disciplined approach to growth as the foundation for sustainable long-term performance and a return to profitability.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Large Warner Bros. holder doesn't believe Paramount bid is superior
data.description:
A top Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD) holder said he doesn't believe that Paramount Skydance's  (PSKY) bid for the entertainment giant is superior to Netflix's (NFLX) deal.

"I think you can quibble about is one offer a few pennies better than the other offer," Bill Nygren, chief investment officer-U.S. of Harris Associates, the No. 5 largest Warner Bros. (WBD) holder told CNBC in an interview on Wednesday. "But to meet the hurdle of substantially signifnicant, I don't think the Paramount bid does that."

Nygren expects that Netflix (NFLX) and Paramount (PSKY) may still raise their bids. 

"We don't think we’ve seen the best bid yet from either company," Nygren said. "Anyone who thinks the current bid is the best bid should sell the stock because the market is anticipating something higher."

"I think either side at this point would be foolish to walk away," Nygren added.
